codeReview.summary: The feature implementation meets the requested behavior: kill now requires explicit YES confirmation, cancel is non-destructive, confirmed kill routes clients to a deterministic fallback workspace, last-session kill is blocked, and launcher/menu fallback parity includes kill. One robustness issue remains around unescaped workspace names in command-prompt/run-shell command construction.

issues.description: Workspace names are interpolated into a single-quoted run-shell command without escaping (also in line 167 with %% substitution). Names containing a single quote can break the confirmation command string, making the kill-confirm prompt path unreliable for valid tmux session names such as ab'cd.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission-level `test` command does not run `tests/workspace-actions.sh`, so kill/fallback regressions are only caught if workers remember to run targeted tests manually.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:20-26 only checks socket/config/key-table smoke; tmux/plugins/tmux-workspace-agent/tests/workspace-actions.sh:102-126 contains the deterministic kill/fallback and last-session safety assertions.

summary: Reviewed feature workspace-kill-confirmation-fallback at commit b332cf0. Implementation satisfies core contract and is acceptable to pass, with one non-blocking quoting robustness gap in the kill confirmation prompt path and one shared-state observation about baseline test coverage in services.yaml.
